creator: Alexander Terp
description:
Map keys are internally hashed as strings. This means int 1, float 1.0, and string "1" all map to the same key. Bool true and string "true" collide too. Creating a map with colliding keys silently loses data.

**Repro:**
```
m = {1: "int", "1": "string"}
print(m)        // {1: "string"} - only one entry, "int" value lost
print(len(m))   // 1

m[1] = "int val"
m["1"] = "str val"
print(m[1])     // "str val" - int key overwritten by string key
```

**Root cause:** `core/type_rad_value.go` line 244-266: Hash() converts int64 via fmt.Sprintf("%d", val) producing "1", same hash as plain string "1". The int/float collision (1 and 1.0) may be intentional, but int/string collision is almost certainly not.

**Test files:** `bugs/operators/91b_map_key_coercion.rad`, `97_map_key_coercion2.rad`, `bugs/collections/108_map_int_str_key_collision.rad`, `109_key_type_collision.rad`, `122_map_key_collision_detail.rad`
title: Map key type coercion causes silent data loss
